summary: Added per-session dollar-loss cap per market slug. When cumulative loss on a slug reaches the threshold, new entries to that slug are banned for the rest of the session.

env_var.name: MAX_SLUG_LOSS_USD
env_var.description: Per-slug dollar loss threshold. When cumulative closed-trade loss on a slug reaches this amount, slug is banned for rest of session. 0 = disabled.

slug_loss_computation.location: bot_core.py bridge loop (pre-gate check after gap_stop bans)
slug_loss_computation.method: SQL: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(pnl_usd), 0) FROM trades WHERE market_slug = ? AND status = 'closed' AND pnl_usd < 0
slug_loss_computation.tracking: In-memory set _session_slug_loss_bans. Persisted to state.json as session_slug_loss_bans and restored on startup.

restart_note: Restart required. Global state (_session_slug_loss_bans) persisted/restored; loss tracking is session-scoped.

implementation_checklist.state_restoration: Lines 406-411: restore session_slug_loss_bans from prior state.json
implementation_checklist.state_persistence: Line 232: add session_slug_loss_bans list to state.json payload
implementation_checklist.bridge_gate_logic: Lines 611-641: two-part check (already banned OR loss cap exceeded)
implementation_checklist.log_messages: Info-level BRIDGE GATE REJECT with exact loss values

scope: Narrow, gate-focused. No risk refactor, dashboard changes, or model changes.
testing_notes: Enable via MAX_SLUG_LOSS_USD=<amount> in .env. Default 0 disables gate. Loss is computed from closed trades only. Loss bans are session-scoped and reset on restart.
